title: Stellan Skarsgård Makes Oscar History with International Film Nomination
text: Stellan Skarsgård has achieved a historic milestone by becoming the first Best Supporting Actor nominee from an international film in the Academy Awards 97-year history. The Swedish actor earned his nomination for Sentimental Value, a Danish-Norwegian production directed by Joachim Trier. In the film, Skarsgård plays Gustav Borg, an estranged filmmaker father attempting to reconnect with his daughters by casting one in his comeback project. While he speaks some English, he primarily converses in Swedish throughout the picture. Though other acting categories have previously recognized international film performers including Fernanda Torres, Antonio Banderas, and Marina de Tavira, the Best Supporting Actor category remained untouched until now. Interestingly, five actors have received nominations for non-English dialogue in American productions, with three winning: Robert De Niro, Benicio Del Toro, and Troy Kotsur. Sentimental Value received nine total Oscar nominations, including Best Picture, Best International Feature, Best Director, and acting nominations for Renate Reinsve, Elle Fanning, and Inga Ibsdotter Lilleaas. Skarsgård competes against Sean Penn, Benicio Del Toro, Jacob Elordi, and Delroy Lindo at the March 15 ceremony.
